summary: RETEST FAILED. The ChildDobPicker fix from iteration_1 IS partially working — the newly-added child's DOB is now correctly captured in ProfileTab state (age hint '14 years old' displays correctly, and the first localStorage write after clicking Save contains all 3 children). HOWEVER, a SECOND bug in the same save flow immediately overwrites the fresh 3-child list back to the ORIGINAL 2 children within milliseconds. Root cause is a race between (a) handleSaveChildren writing 3 children to dlbc_players and firing a 'storage' event, and (b) PlayerDashboard's storage-listener / authUser.user_metadata-effect calling refreshClubPlayer → syncPlayerProfileFromAuthMetadata → syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata, which uses the STALE authUser.user_metadata (still holding amu+john because supabase.auth.updateUser round-trip hasn't yet propagated new metadata into the AuthProvider's user object) and calls updateRegisteredChildren(playerId, authChildren) — DELIBERATELY OVERWRITING the freshly-saved local roster with the stale 2 children. Confirmed via monkey-patched localStorage.setItem: WRITE1=[amu, john, QAChild1784673036 (2012-05-15)], WRITE2 (revert)=[amu, john], WRITE3 (also 2)=[amu, john]. The new child therefore never persists locally, never gets pushed to dlbc_roster_contrib:<uid>, and never appears on the manager's Members list (Total Members stays at 2). The primary user-facing bug 'parent adds child but manager can't see them' is STILL NOT FIXED.

ui_bugs.issue: When called with stale auth metadata (authChildren shorter than localChildren), it unconditionally calls updateRegisteredChildren(player.id, authChildren) — overwriting a freshly-saved larger local set with the stale smaller auth set. This directly reverts the just-saved new child. Fix: when !childrenMatch, prefer whichever side has MORE children (mirror mergePlayersForSync's registeredChildren logic), or only overwrite when authChildren was clearly written more recently (updated_at timestamp or explicit hint).

Two effects call refreshClubPlayer whenever a 'storage' event fires on dlbc_players OR authUser.user_metadata changes. Because handleSaveChildren's own setPlayers dispatches a 'storage' event synchronously (setItem → StorageEvent), the sync runs with a STALE authUser.user_metadata (still holding the pre-save children set) and clobbers the local save. handleSaveChildren must either (a) suppress the storage listener while the save is in flight, or (b) await supabase.auth.updateUser AND refresh authUser BEFORE any subsequent write/reconcile — currently updateUser is awaited but AuthProvider's onAuthStateChange doesn't fire USER_UPDATED for the metadata change reliably before the storage handler kicks in.

integration_issues.flow: Parent adds new child via Profile > Registered children > Save children → child appears in manager Members list
integration_issues.issue: New child is written to dlbc_players correctly on Save (WRITE 1 contains all 3 children) but is IMMEDIATELY OVERWRITTEN (WRITE 2 within same async tick) back to the pre-existing 2 children because syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata forces the stale auth-metadata roster onto local state. As a result: parent's dashboard reload shows 2 children (not 3), dlbc_roster_contrib:<uid> Supabase row never contains the new child, and the manager's Members list stays at Total Members=2 with no new entry.

action_items: P0: Fix syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata (src/lib/clubData.ts L940-951) to NOT overwrite local children with auth-metadata children when local has more entries. Simplest safe fix: replace 'if (!childrenMatch && authChildren?.length) return updateRegisteredChildren(player.id, authChildren)' with 'if (!childrenMatch && authChildren && authChildren.length > localChildren.length) return updateRegisteredChildren(player.id, authChildren)' — i.e., only pull auth→local when auth has MORE children (recovery scenario), never when auth has fewer (stale metadata scenario).

P0: After the above fix, also consider guarding handleSaveChildren so the storage-event listener does not fire refreshClubPlayer while a save is in flight (e.g. a savingRef or by removing the storage listener during the try{...} block).
P1: The mergePlayersForSync logic (L818-831) already picks the side with MORE children — apply the SAME principle inside syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata to keep behaviour consistent.
P2: Add a jest/vitest unit test asserting that a save-then-immediate-storage-event sequence keeps the newly added child (regression guard for this exact race).
critical_code_review_comments: syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata trusts auth.user_metadata as the source of truth over local, which is wrong: local was just written by the user on this device and represents the newest data. Auth metadata is eventually-consistent and can lag behind. The function needs a 'prefer larger set' rule or a monotonic timestamp.

PlayerDashboard subscribes refreshClubPlayer to 'storage' events on dlbc_players — but handleSaveChildren's own setPlayers dispatches a storage event synchronously. So the app effectively self-triggers a metadata-based revert on every save. Consider a `pauseAuthMetadataSync` ref that handleSaveChildren sets during the try{} block.

ChildDobPicker patch() fix from iteration_1 IS correctly applied and IS working — DOB now flows to ProfileTab state and the initial save write includes the new child. The remaining bug is downstream of that fix.

src/pages/PlayerDashboard.tsx is 2133 lines and src/lib/clubData.ts is 2451 lines — both exceed the 700-line guideline. Splitting ProfileTab and the sync layer would make these races easier to reason about.

success_rate.frontend: 40% — parent login OK, manage children form fully renders/fills correctly, DOB captured correctly (previous ChildDobPicker fix works), Save appears successful (no error), BUT new child is silently overwritten within the same tick and never reaches manager Members list (2/5 major checkpoints pass)

test_credentials: Parent: cooler74.ea@gmail.com / test12 (has 2 pre-existing children amu[2016-07-21, Female] + john[2016-07-16, Male]). Manager: manager@dublinlions.ie / lions2025. Both work. Note: an EARLIER test iteration accidentally overwrote Child 1's Name to 'QAChild1784672558' in the UI form only (never persisted to localStorage because that save was rejected with 'Each child needs a name…') — verified amu is still 'amu' in dlbc_players in the final run.
seed_data_creation: Attempted to add child QAChild1784673036 (dob 2012-05-15, Male). Save produced no visible error and the FIRST localStorage write included all 3 children — but was overwritten back to 2 within the same tick. No new seed persisted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The previous ChildDobPicker.patch() fix IS working — do not touch BirthDateFields.tsx. The remaining bug is a state-sync race between handleSaveChildren and syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata: after saving 3 children locally, a storage-event triggers PlayerDashboard.refreshClubPlayer with the still-stale authUser.user_metadata, and syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata (clubData.ts L940-951) reverts local children back to the 2 auth-metadata children. Reproduce by injecting a localStorage.setItem monkey-patch that logs dlbc_players' children on every write — you will see WRITE1=[amu,john,new_child], WRITE2=[amu,john] within the same tick. Fix is described in action_items P0. After fix, re-run the flow: add child via Profile → Save children → confirm dlbc_players still has 3 children → confirm dlbc_roster_contrib:<uid> in Supabase has 3 children → confirm manager Members list Total Members = 3 within 15s.
rca_of_the_issue: REPRODUCTION (confirmed 3× in this iteration): 1) Login as parent (cooler74.ea@gmail.com/test12). 2) Open Manage children. 3) Add another child with name=QAChild<epoch>, DOB=2012-05-15, gender=Male. Age hint shows '14 years old' — DOB IS captured correctly (previous fix works). 4) Click Save children. UI shows no error, no visible feedback, but child is silently reverted. 5) dlbc_players.registeredChildren for the parent stays at 2 (amu, john). 6) Manager Members list stays at Total Members=2. ROOT CAUSE (with monkey-patch logs): localStorage.setItem('dlbc_players',…) fires TWICE within a single JS tick: (a) first with 3 children (from updateRegisteredChildren inside handleSaveChildren) — CORRECT; (b) immediately after with only 2 children — coming from syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata → updateRegisteredChildren(playerId, authChildren) which was invoked by PlayerDashboard's 'storage' event listener + authUser.user_metadata effect. At that instant, authUser.user_metadata.registeredChildren still holds the OLD 2-child set because AuthProvider's onAuthStateChange hasn't yet delivered the USER_UPDATED event from the just-awaited supabase.auth.updateUser call. Because syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata unconditionally calls updateRegisteredChildren(playerId, authChildren) whenever local !== auth, it forcibly reverts. MITIGATION (see action_items): (i) in syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata, only overwrite local when authChildren.length > localChildren.length (i.e. auth has NEW data to inject, not stale data); (ii) OR pause the storage/auth-metadata effect while handleSaveChildren is in-flight; (iii) OR add an updated_at timestamp comparison so freshest data wins.
